subject: A Review of Set Theory
question: Find the power set of $A = \{a, b, c\}$.
explanation: $\mathcal{P}(A) = \{\emptyset, \{a\}, \{b\}, \{c\}, \{a,b\}, \{a,c\}, \{b,c\}, \{a,b,c\}\}$ We conclude this section, by introducing the concept of mathematical induction: We want to prove that some statement $P(n)$ is true for any non-negative integer $n \ge n_0$. The steps of mathematical induction are as follows: (i) (Basis of induction) Show that $P(n_0)$ is true. (ii) (Induction hypothesis) Assume $P(n_0), P(n_0 + 1), \ldots, P(n)$ are true. (iii) (Induction step) Show that $P(n + 1)$ is true.
